title: Universal Spawn Manifest
description: Normative JSON Schema for a universal-spawn v1.0 manifest. A single declarative file describing what a creation is, where it runs, what it needs, and what it promises.
$comment: This schema is draft-07 intentionally. Draft-07 has the broadest validator support across ecosystems (Ajv, python-jsonschema, go-jsonschema, rust-jsonschema, swift-json-schema). A v1.1 draft-2020-12 companion is tracked under spec/v1.0.0/.

anyOf.$comment: Either list at least one platform extension...
...or a deployment target set. A manifest with neither has nothing for any consumer to act on.

version.description: Version of the universal-spawn spec this manifest targets. Only '1.0' is accepted by this schema.
version.$comment: Consumers MUST reject a manifest whose major version they do not implement. Unlike semver packages, spec versions are tightly bounded enums so validators cannot 'do their best'.

name.description: Human-readable display name. Alphanumerics, space, dot, underscore, dash. No leading or trailing whitespace. No emoji.
name.$comment: We accept spaces so real product names render correctly ('Hello World', 'Research Agent'). We reject emoji via lint because JSON Schema regex cannot portably express the Unicode property escape needed to detect them; the spec prose forbids emoji explicitly.

description.description: One paragraph, 10-500 characters. First sentence should stand alone as a summary; indexers use it as a snippet.
description.$comment: The lower bound exists to discourage placeholder descriptions. The upper bound exists so description fits in a card.

type.description: Primary category of this creation. Determines which platforms can spawn it and what fields are expected.
type.$comment: Additions happen only on minor spec bumps. Removals never.

summary.description: Optional one-line summary for social cards. <=140 characters.
summary.$comment: Sized for the narrowest card surface; longer text is silently truncated by consumers.

platforms.description: Platform-specific extensions. Each key is a platform id (claude, gemini, openai, vercel, netlify, unity, figma, discord, huggingface, ...). Each value is validated separately by that platform's extension schema.
platforms.$comment: Intentionally permissive at this level — the platform's own schema.extension.json is where strict validation happens. This keeps the master schema decoupled from the platform list.

safety.description: Declarative safety envelope. The platform enforces; the manifest declares.

min_permissions.description: Smallest set of capabilities this creation needs to function.

items.$comment: Format: 'namespace:action' or 'namespace:action:scope'. Scope accepts hostnames, filesystem paths, and key=value identifiers.

rate_limit_qps.description: Self-declared maximum outbound request rate, queries per second.

cost_limit_usd_daily.description: Self-declared upper bound on daily USD cost.

safe_for_auto_spawn.description: True only if the author certifies this manifest is safe to spawn without human confirmation under min_permissions and the declared limits.

data_residency.description: Regions where data generated by this creation may legally reside.

deployment.description: How this creation deploys, if it deploys as a service.

targets.description: Ordered preference list. First entry is the canonical target.

engine.description: Runtime or framework engine (node, python, rust, docker, unity, ...)

env_vars_required.description: Environment variables the creation needs. Declare by NAME only; never a value.

name.description: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. First character A-Z, remainder A-Z0-9_.

items.$comment: Secret values are never stored in a manifest; they are supplied by the platform's credential store at spawn time.

visuals.description: Visual assets. Following the Residual Frequencies visual system is recommended but not required.

id.$comment: Reverse-DNS ids survive renames, forks, and platform migrations. Recommended but not required; the repo URL + commit in metadata.source is sufficient for uniqueness.

x-ext.description: Free-form escape hatch. Keys MUST begin with a reverse-DNS vendor prefix. Consumers MAY ignore all x-ext content.
